trade.summary: Bilateral trade is moderate, with Canada importing iron ore, soybeans, and aircraft components from Brazil. Canada exports mining equipment, aerospace parts, and agricultural technology.

diplomatic.summary: Brazil and Canada maintain friendly diplomatic relations with shared interests in multilateral institutions. Both are G20 members and cooperate on environmental and trade issues, though they are not treaty allies.

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Limited defense cooperation. Both countries engage through UN peacekeeping frameworks.

financial.summary: Canadian mining companies (including Vale predecessor investments) have significant presence in Brazil. FDI flows in both directions, particularly in extractive industries.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Some Canadian involvement in Brazilian oil sector. Both are major energy producers with overlapping interests in clean energy transition.

scientific.summary: Growing academic and research collaboration, particularly in agriculture, forestry, and environmental science. Several joint university programs exist.
